summary: Taj & Bro is a New Jersey/New York metro labor-only moving company operating since 2011. The customer rents their own truck (U-Haul, Penske, etc.) and Taj & Bro supplies an experienced, insured crew to load, unload, and move heavy items, including specialized piano moving. It serves budget-conscious residential movers, piano owners, and commercial/storage clients on a one-off, quote-based hourly basis.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Pre-Move Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Confirm the booking instantly, set move-day expectations, and reduce pre-move anxiety so the customer shows up prepared and the job runs smoothly.
campaigns.trigger: Job booked / quote accepted (verbal or form). Email 1 fires within seconds of the booking being confirmed.
campaigns.segment: Newly booked customers; branch by move type (residential, piano, commercial).
campaigns.rationale: Everything is missing and this is the fastest, highest-engagement win — for a same-day urgency purchase, an instant confirmation builds trust before the crew arrives and cuts day-of confusion that leads to no-shows.

emails.purpose: Instant booking confirmation that delivers a first win (a simple move-day prep checklist), reassures on trust, and sets expectations for what happens next.
emails.subject: You're locked in — here's exactly how move day will go
emails.preview: Have the truck ready. We handle every pound of the rest.
keyPoints: Confirm the date, arrival window, and crew size so there are zero surprises
One quick win: a short prep checklist (park the truck close, clear the path, label fragile/piano items)
Trust reassurance: experienced, insured crew lifting since 2011 — not random day-laborers
Set expectations: your only job is to have the truck ready; reply to this email or call (908) 457-3846 with anything

campaigns.name: Quote-to-Booking Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert a free quote that didn't book same-day into a confirmed job by killing the trust objection and creating real date-availability urgency.
campaigns.trigger: Quote requested (phone or form) but not booked within ~24 hours.
campaigns.segment: Open quotes with no confirmed date; branch by move type, leaning hard into piano for piano leads.
campaigns.rationale: The free quote is this brand's 'trial,' and the core revenue gap is quote requests that go cold with zero follow-up — one well-timed email that answers 'can I trust them and will they show up?' is the highest-leverage send.

emails.purpose: Re-open the quote, dismantle the #1 objection (trusting strangers with valuables, will they show up), and push to lock the date before weekend slots fill.
emails.subject: Your quote's still good — but your date won't wait
emails.preview: Insured, lifting since 2011, and we actually show up. Lock your crew.
keyPoints: Answer the trust objection head-on: insured, specialized piano handling, real crew with a track record since 2011
Address 'will they show up?' directly — confirmed crew, confirmed time, no ghosting
Urgency: weekend and month-end move dates fill fast; reserve the slot now
Counter the 'just ask friends' objection — one dropped piano or thrown-out back costs more than the labor
One-tap path to confirm: reply or call (908) 457-3846

campaigns.name: Booking Reminders & No-Show Prevention
campaigns.objective: Maximize attended, on-time jobs by confirming the crew, sharing day-of logistics, and giving an easy reschedule path instead of a silent no-show.
campaigns.trigger: Confirmed job with an upcoming move date; reminder fires 24 hours before.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled move; reference the specific move type and crew lead.
campaigns.rationale: This is an appointment-run business where a missed or confused move day is lost revenue on both sides — reminders cut no-shows ~35% on average and prevent day-of chaos (truck not ready, wrong address, blocked access).

emails.purpose: Day-before reminder that confirms arrival time, lists last-minute prep, and offers a frictionless reschedule link to convert would-be no-shows into rebooks.
emails.subject: See you tomorrow — your crew arrives at [arrival time]
emails.preview: A 2-minute checklist so we hit the ground lifting.
keyPoints: Restate the arrival window, crew size, and crew lead's name so they know who's knocking
Final prep checklist: truck parked and ready, walkway clear, fragile items and piano flagged
Direct line for day-of questions or a blocked entrance: (908) 457-3846
One-click reschedule link — a moved date beats a no-show for both sides

campaigns.name: Review & Referral
campaigns.objective: Capture post-move goodwill into a public review and a warm referral while the relief of a damage-free move is fresh.
campaigns.trigger: Job completed; email fires the day after a successful move (peak-goodwill value moment).
campaigns.segment: Customers whose move just finished; weight piano/commercial clients for higher-value referrals.
campaigns.rationale: Referral velocity is a stated primary goal and there's no structured ask today — residential moves are one-and-done, so the only durable growth from a happy customer is the review and the friend they know who's also moving.

emails.purpose: Thank the customer, ask for a quick review on the platforms that drive new leads, and prompt a referral with a clear both-sides reason to share.
emails.subject: Nothing scratched, right? Do us one quick favor
emails.preview: 60 seconds of your time keeps the next mover's piano safe too.
keyPoints: Warm thank-you that names the outcome — everything arrived in one piece, stress gone
Ask for a short review where it counts: Google and U-Haul MovingHelp
Referral ask: everyone knows someone moving soon — send them our way
Make sharing effortless with the phone number and a forwardable line; mention a thank-you for referrals that book

campaigns.name: Win-Back & Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate past customers who may be moving again (or know someone who is) and recover quote leads that went cold, while keeping the list healthy.
campaigns.trigger: 12+ months since last move (likely next-move window) or a quote lead dormant 60–90 days.
campaigns.segment: Past residential customers near a likely next move; separately, never-booked cold quote leads.
campaigns.rationale: Platform bookings and one-and-done residential moves mean most customers never return organically — a light periodic nudge recovers 5–15% of a list that would otherwise go fully dark.

emails.purpose: Re-open the relationship with a low-pressure 'moving again, or know someone who is?' nudge that points to a fast quote and an easy referral.
emails.subject: Moving again? Or know someone who is?
emails.preview: Same crew, still lifting since 2011. Fast quote, easy date.
keyPoints: Reconnect with a friendly, no-pressure check-in tied to the likely next-move window
Remind them why they hired us: damage-free, on-time, specialized piano handling
Two easy paths: book your own next move, or refer a friend who's moving
Frictionless quote via form or (908) 457-3846; soft sunset for anyone who stays unresponsive
